roles.label: NHSN dQM Implementation Coordinator
roles.description: Coordinates facility preparation for FHIR dQM reporting, connects stakeholders, educates key personnel, and supports ongoing readiness for NHSNLink reporting.
roles.answerGuidance: Frame answers around coordination tasks, stakeholder communication, implementation readiness, and what needs to be assigned or verified across teams.

roles.label: NHSN Facility Administrator
roles.description: Responsible for NHSN facility-level administrative tasks, including monthly Digital Reporting Plan review/signoff, user management, and ensuring facility locations are correctly mapped.
roles.answerGuidance: Emphasize operational signoff, NHSN reporting readiness, location mapping, and what requires facility-level administrative action.

roles.label: IT - EHR Vendor Team
roles.description: Reviews the NHSN dQM IG, confirms FHIR R4 API availability, verifies US Core conformance, and coordinates EHR/FHIR server readiness.
roles.answerGuidance: Provide more technical detail about FHIR resources, profiles, APIs, conformance, validation, and implementation impact.

roles.label: IT - Laboratory Data Team
roles.description: Ensures laboratory tests and results are mapped to appropriate standard terminologies such as LOINC and SNOMED and maintains coding updates.
roles.answerGuidance: Prioritize terminology bindings, lab test/result mapping, LOINC, SNOMED, and data quality implications.

roles.label: IT - Medication Data Team
roles.description: Ensures medications and medication routes are mapped to appropriate terminologies such as RxNorm and SNOMED, including third-party pharmacy data where applicable.
roles.answerGuidance: Prioritize medication resources, RxNorm, SNOMED, MedicationRequest, MedicationAdministration, and mapping/data maintenance implications.

roles.label: Executive Suite - Informatics
roles.description: Provides executive approval and designates teams responsible for oversight of FHIR dQM reporting preparation.
roles.answerGuidance: Keep answers concise, strategic, and focused on readiness, risk, governance, resource needs, and decision points.

roles.label: Executive Suite - Quality/Safety
roles.description: Provides executive approval and designates clinical quality/safety teams responsible for oversight of FHIR dQM reporting activities.
roles.answerGuidance: Frame answers around quality reporting, clinical oversight, reporting impact, accountability, and readiness.
